areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: Implements Audio Token Selection using the last layer of the audio encoder: computes A = Softmax(QK^T/√d) (Eq. 2), derives the per-token mea
leaves.why: omnizip.py audio_attention_scores computes softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) and the other-token mean (col_sum-diag)/(Na-1), and select_salient_audio takes top rho_a% (Eq.2), but the explicit same-average-pooling alignment step is not present.

leaves.label: Implements the audio-guided dynamic video pruning rate ρ'_v(i) = ρmax − (ρmax − ρmin)·Sa(i) (Eq. 5) with ρmax=0.75 and ρmin=0.35, where high
leaves.why: omnizip.py dynamic_video_rates computes rho_prime = cfg.rho_max-(cfg.rho_max-cfg.rho_min)*Sa with OmniZipConfig rho_max=0.75/rho_min=0.35, then scales rho_prime by target_pruned/cur_pruned to honor the global rho_v budget (Eq.5).

leaves.label: Implements Audio Anchor Consolidation: uniformly samples anchors from non-salient audio tokens, computes cross-modal similarity S_cross = Ĥa
leaves.why: omnizip.py implements uniform anchor sampling (torch.randperm), cross_modal_similarity with l2_row_normalize using EPS=1e-6 (Eq.3/4), and G=15 for AVUT else 3 (from_retention), but the top-G merge is not anchor-specific.

leaves.label: Implements the ISTC block with a minimum processing unit of four frames: computes inter-frame cosine similarity S_vid (Eq. 6) to temporally
leaves.why: omnizip.py istc_block uses frames_per_unit=4, temporal_merge_indices via F.cosine_similarity on frames 2&4 (Eq.6), and dpc_knn_density_scores with k=5 computing rho=exp(-mean(knn^2)) (Eq.7), delta (Eq.8) and score delta*rho on frames 1&3.

leaves.label: Re-implements all comparison baselines on the same backbone: Random pruning (control), FastV using the L-th layer attention matrix with the
leaves.why: omnizip.py random_prune implements the random control (measured as random_control_45 in metrics.json) but FastV/DyCoke/VisionZip are not implemented and metrics.json lists FastV only as out_of_scope.

leaves.label: Evaluates on the four named audio-video benchmarks: AVUT/AVUTBench (six tasks event localization EL, object matching OM, OCR matching OR, in
leaves.why: data_unavailable: dataset declared in data_load_failures or scope.gaps

leaves.label: Applies the paper's frame caps: maximum 768 frames for the relatively long VideoMME videos and 128 frames for all other datasets, as specifi
leaves.why: config.frame_caps={worldsense:128,...,videomme:768} and qwen_omni_runner.build_inputs reads max_frames default 128 (the value used for the measured WorldSense run); the 768 path is configured but not exercised.

leaves.label: Constructs the audio-video token sequence with fixed-length time windows containing 50 audio tokens and 288 video tokens per window, with co
leaves.why: omnizip.py compress_sequence windows the sequence with aw=cfg.audio_per_window=50 and vw=cfg.video_per_window=288 per window (config.hparams audio_tokens_per_window=50, video_tokens_per_window=288), aligning audio/video by window index.

leaves.label: Runs OmniZip on both Qwen2.5-Omni-7B and Qwen2.5-Omni-3B backbones as the OmniLLM under test (Section 4.1, Section 4.2).
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model contains full results for both qwen2_5_omni_7b and qwen2_5_omni_3b and scope.models_run lists both backbones.

leaves.label: Executes the two reported retention-rate configurations: 45% retained ratio with ρa=0.3, ρv=0.6 and 35% retained ratio with ρa=0.4, ρv=0.7 (
leaves.why: data_unavailable: dataset declared in data_load_failures or scope.gaps

leaves.label: Runs inference on an NVIDIA A6000 48GB GPU with FlashAttention enabled to reduce memory, matching the Section 4.1 hardware/optimization setu
leaves.why: metrics.json env and provenance.json hardware both record gpu='NVIDIA RTX A5000' with attn_impl='sdpa', contradicting the A6000 48GB + FlashAttention requirement.

leaves.label: Uses LMMs-Eval for VideoMME evaluation and the unified testing code for the remaining benchmarks, as specified in Section 4.2.
leaves.why: data.py and qwen_omni_runner.py implement a bespoke loader/runner; no LMMs-Eval import or VideoMME evaluation appears in evidence.

leaves.label: Uses the overall FLOPs ratio (counting only multimodal audio+video tokens) as the metric to set pruning ratios across methods for fair compa
leaves.why: mechanism_evidence._prefill_flops counts attention+proj+mlp FLOPs for speedup reporting, but the runs set retention via cfg.from_retention(0.45/0.35) and the random control is matched by token budget, not FLOPs ratio.

leaves.label: Reports the four efficiency metrics from Table 3 on WorldSense: GPU memory, prefilling time (with speedup factor), accuracy, and latency per
leaves.why: metrics.json worldsense entries report peak_mem_gb/gpu_memory_gb, mean_prefill_ms+prefilling_speedup, accuracy, and mean_latency_s for full_baseline and retention configs — all four Table-3 metrics — but on A5000 hardware.

leaves.label: Normalizes Table 1 omnimodal QA results as percentages with the full-token baseline accuracy set to 100%, and reports the AVUT six-task (EL/
leaves.why: accuracy_normalized (e.g. 0.9506 = 38.5/40.5) implements baseline=100% normalization for WorldSense, but the Table-1 AVUT six-task and VideoMME columns are not present and those datasets were not run.

leaves.label: Reports per-domain WorldSense scores across the eight domains (Tech & Science, Culture & Politics, Daily Life, Film & TV, Performance, Games
leaves.why: metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_r45_per_domain and worldsense_full_baseline_per_domain report all eight domains (Tech & Science, Culture & Politics, Daily Life, Film & TV, Performance, Games, Sports, Music) plus 'average' and n_domains=8.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduces WorldSense Qwen2.5-Omni-7B averages within tolerance: OmniZip 45% retained = 45.9 and 35% retained = 45.3 versus Full Tokens 46.8
leaves.why: Measured 7B WorldSense full_baseline=40.5, retention_45=38.5, retention_35=38.5 (accuracy_normalized 0.9506) preserves the paper's near-baseline-retention trend within tolerance, but the cross-method 50%-retention comparison cannot be checked.

leaves.label: Reproduces the 7B efficiency targets from Table 3: 3.42× prefilling speedup (85ms vs 291ms) and 1.42× overall latency speedup at 35%, 2.51×
leaves.why: metrics.json 7B worldsense gives retention_45 prefilling_speedup=2.447 (paper 2.51×) and retention_35=3.025 (paper 3.42×), full_baseline peak_mem_gb=27.0 vs 21.666 (35%)/22.522 (45%), and latency 4.236s→0.827s — all trends (faster prefill, lower memory, lower latency) agree with

leaves.label: Reproduces Table 1 Qwen2.5-Omni-7B at 45% retained ratio: 99.1% normalized average score and AVUTBench average of 63.0 versus full-token 64.
leaves.why: metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_r45_accuracy_normalized=0.9506 shows near-full retention direction, but the AVUTBench average the leaf names is absent (scope.out_of_scope: 'AVUT full run').

leaves.label: Reproduces the Qwen2.5-Omni-3B results: 3.27× prefilling speedup at 35% (Table 3) and WorldSense OmniZip 45% average = 45.2 versus Full Toke
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.qwen2_5_omni_3b.retention_35.prefilling_speedup=3.179 (paper 3.27×) and WorldSense 45% accuracy 42.5 vs full_baseline 45.5 — the same below-full ordering Table 2 reports (45.2 vs 46.4).

leaves.label: Confirms the OmniZip pruning step adds less than 40 ms of runtime overhead, as claimed in Section 3.5.
leaves.why: metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_mean_prune_ms=374.8588 (and per-arm mean_prune_ms ~363–381ms) is the measured compress_sequence overhead and far exceeds the 40ms bound, so the claim is not confirmed.

leaves.label: Provides a training-free, inference-time codebase (no training/fine-tuning scripts) consistent with the released repository github.com/KD-TA
leaves.why: omnizip.py implements a training-free inference-time compressor and qwen_omni_runner.py runs inference only (config.method='OmniZip (training-free token compression)'), but the code listing also ships generic training scripts copied from a prior attempt.

leaves.label: Includes the ablation reproductions: Table 5 isolating dynamic video pruning (DP) and audio anchor consolidation (AC) on Qwen2.5-Omni-7B, an
leaves.why: metrics.json ablation_dp_only_45 (37.0) and ablation_ac_only_45 (38.5) plus config.ablations cover Table 5 DP/AC isolation, and random_control_45 covers Random, but the Table 4 ISTC-vs-DyCoke-vs-VisionZip token-selection comparison is absent.

leaves.label: Documents the full hyperparameter configuration in a config/readme artifact: ρmax=0.75, ρmin=0.35, k=5, G=15 (AVUT)/G=3 (others), and the (ρ
leaves.why: metrics.json config.hparams documents rho_max=0.75, rho_min=0.35, k=5, G_general=3, G_AVUT=15, retention_45={rho_a:0.3,rho_v:0.6}, retention_35={rho_a:0.4,rho_v:0.7}, matching omnizip.py OmniZipConfig.from_retention exactly.

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json 7B worldsense gives retention_45 prefilling_speedup=2.447 (paper 2.51×) and retention_35=3.025 (paper 3.42×), full_baseline peak_mem_gb=27.0 vs 21.666 (35%)/22.522 (45%), and latency 4.236s→0.827s — all trends (faster prefill, lower memory, lower latency) agree with Table 3 directions.
metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_r45_accuracy_normalized=0.9506 shows near-full retention direction, but the AVUTBench average the leaf names is absent (scope.out_of_scope: 'AVUT full run').

metrics.json per_model.qwen2_5_omni_3b.retention_35.prefilling_speedup=3.179 (paper 3.27×) and WorldSense 45% accuracy 42.5 vs full_baseline 45.5 — the same below-full ordering Table 2 reports (45.2 vs 46.4).
metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_mean_prune_ms=374.8588 (and per-arm mean_prune_ms ~363–381ms) is the measured compress_sequence overhead and far exceeds the 40ms bound, so the claim is not confirmed.

omnizip.py implements a training-free inference-time compressor and qwen_omni_runner.py runs inference only (config.method='OmniZip (training-free token compression)'), but the code listing also ships generic training scripts copied from a prior attempt.

metrics.json ablation_dp_only_45 (37.0) and ablation_ac_only_45 (38.5) plus config.ablations cover Table 5 DP/AC isolation, and random_control_45 covers Random, but the Table 4 ISTC-vs-DyCoke-vs-VisionZip token-selection comparison is absent.

metrics.json config.hparams documents rho_max=0.75, rho_min=0.35, k=5, G_general=3, G_AVUT=15, retention_45={rho_a:0.3,rho_v:0.6}, retention_35={rho_a:0.4,rho_v:0.7}, matching omnizip.py OmniZipConfig.from_retention exactly.

omnizip.py audio_attention_scores computes softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) and the other-token mean (col_sum-diag)/(Na-1), and select_salient_audio takes top rho_a% (Eq.2), but the explicit same-average-pooling alignment step is not present.

omnizip.py dynamic_video_rates computes rho_prime = cfg.rho_max-(cfg.rho_max-cfg.rho_min)*Sa with OmniZipConfig rho_max=0.75/rho_min=0.35, then scales rho_prime by target_pruned/cur_pruned to honor the global rho_v budget (Eq.5).

omnizip.py implements uniform anchor sampling (torch.randperm), cross_modal_similarity with l2_row_normalize using EPS=1e-6 (Eq.3/4), and G=15 for AVUT else 3 (from_retention), but the top-G merge is not anchor-specific.

omnizip.py istc_block uses frames_per_unit=4, temporal_merge_indices via F.cosine_similarity on frames 2&4 (Eq.6), and dpc_knn_density_scores with k=5 computing rho=exp(-mean(knn^2)) (Eq.7), delta (Eq.8) and score delta*rho on frames 1&3.
omnizip.py random_prune implements the random control (measured as random_control_45 in metrics.json) but FastV/DyCoke/VisionZip are not implemented and metrics.json lists FastV only as out_of_scope.

config.frame_caps={worldsense:128,...,videomme:768} and qwen_omni_runner.build_inputs reads max_frames default 128 (the value used for the measured WorldSense run); the 768 path is configured but not exercised.

omnizip.py compress_sequence windows the sequence with aw=cfg.audio_per_window=50 and vw=cfg.video_per_window=288 per window (config.hparams audio_tokens_per_window=50, video_tokens_per_window=288), aligning audio/video by window index.
metrics.json per_model contains full results for both qwen2_5_omni_7b and qwen2_5_omni_3b and scope.models_run lists both backbones.
metrics.json env and provenance.json hardware both record gpu='NVIDIA RTX A5000' with attn_impl='sdpa', contradicting the A6000 48GB + FlashAttention requirement.
data.py and qwen_omni_runner.py implement a bespoke loader/runner; no LMMs-Eval import or VideoMME evaluation appears in evidence.

mechanism_evidence._prefill_flops counts attention+proj+mlp FLOPs for speedup reporting, but the runs set retention via cfg.from_retention(0.45/0.35) and the random control is matched by token budget, not FLOPs ratio.

metrics.json worldsense entries report peak_mem_gb/gpu_memory_gb, mean_prefill_ms+prefilling_speedup, accuracy, and mean_latency_s for full_baseline and retention configs — all four Table-3 metrics — but on A5000 hardware.

accuracy_normalized (e.g. 0.9506 = 38.5/40.5) implements baseline=100% normalization for WorldSense, but the Table-1 AVUT six-task and VideoMME columns are not present and those datasets were not run.

metrics.json worldsense_omnizip_r45_per_domain and worldsense_full_baseline_per_domain report all eight domains (Tech & Science, Culture & Politics, Daily Life, Film & TV, Performance, Games, Sports, Music) plus 'average' and n_domains=8.

Measured 7B WorldSense full_baseline=40.5, retention_45=38.5, retention_35=38.5 (accuracy_normalized 0.9506) preserves the paper's near-baseline-retention trend within tolerance, but the cross-method 50%-retention comparison cannot be checked.
